prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio 0.711) and extreme seasonality capture variance (1.0858), with early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (FT_svr). The true series shows mixed trend directions (up, up, down) across folds. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is optimal as it can leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "DWT_svr", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ARIMA", "CWT_svr", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.711,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio 0.711) and extreme seasonality capture variance (1.0858), with early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (FT_svr). The true series shows mixed trend directions (up, up, down) across folds. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is optimal as it can leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach."
}

{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set is diverse (2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5; 1 baseline: dba_combination; 2 weighted: exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboard shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE=2887.11) beats exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 (RMSE=2889.17), but current set already has diverse types and sufficient candidates. No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). Score margin between top candidates is small (0.004). All candidates in valid_candidate_names. No changes needed.",

}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k8", "inverse_rmse_weights_k8_sh0.70"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE: 2887.11) beats current best exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 (RMSE: 2889.17). inverse_rmse_weights_k8_sh0.70 (SMAPE: 0.0637) beats current inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (SMAPE: 0.0664). n_unique_winners=11≥3 supports per-horizon selection diversity. relative_spread_mean=0.13<0.25 so disagreement is moderate - no need for DBA/trimmed_mean additions.", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 for better RMSE", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k8_sh0.70 for better SMAPE"], "when_good": "candidate set has diverse methods (selection, weighted, DBA) and n_windows=3 requires stronger shrinkage via k8 variants"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (RMSE spread ratio 0.711) and extreme seasonality capture variance (1.0858), with early horizon specialists (ETS, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (FT_svr). The true series shows mixed trend directions (up, up, down) across folds. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is optimal as it can leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=11 indicates high model diversity across horizons. RMSE spread ratio=0.711 suggests significant model disagreement. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 provides stability. Selection methods (best_per_horizon, topk_mean_k5) capture horizon-specific specialists like ETS/THETA (early) vs FT_svr/DWT_rf (late). Weighted methods with trim_ratio=1.0 reduce sensitivity to outliers in few-window regime.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set is diverse (2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5; 1 baseline: dba_combination; 2 weighted: exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboard shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE=2887.11) beats exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 (RMSE=2889.17), but current set already has diverse types and sufficient candidates. No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). Score margin between top candidates is small (0.004). All candidates in valid_candidate_names. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. topk_mean_per_horizon_k8 (RMSE: 2887.11) beats current best exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 (RMSE: 2889.17). inverse_rmse_weights_k8_sh0.70 (SMAPE: 0.0637) beats current inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (SMAPE: 0.0664). n_unique_winners=11≥3 supports per-horizon selection diversity. relative_spread_mean=0.13<0.25 so disagreement is moderate - no need for DBA/trimmed_mean additions.
statistician.when_good: candidate set has diverse methods (selection, weighted, DBA) and n_windows=3 requires stronger shrinkage via k8 variants
